user.title: Configure ESC/POS Bluetooth Printer
user.description: Enter the Bluetooth MAC address of your thermal printer and the RFCOMM channel (usually 1).

data_description.mac_address: Format: AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF — find the MAC on the printer self-test printout
data_description.rfcomm_channel: Usually 1. Try 2 if the connection fails.

test_connection.title: Test Bluetooth Connection
test_connection.description:
Press **Next** to test the connection to printer `{mac}` on channel `{channel}`.

⚠️ Make sure the printer is **powered on** and the LED is **blinking** (discoverable mode).

bluetooth_pairing_guide.title: ⚠️ Printer Not Paired — Follow These Steps
bluetooth_pairing_guide.description:
Printer `{mac}` is not yet paired with Home Assistant.

**Step 1** — Open the **HA Terminal** (Settings → Add-ons → Terminal & SSH)

**Step 2** — Run these commands one by one:

```
bluetoothctl
power on
agent on
default-agent
scan on
```

Wait for `{mac}` to appear in the list, then:

```
scan off
{cmd_pair}
```

If prompted for a PIN, enter: `{pin}`

Finally:

```
{cmd_trust}
quit
```

**Step 3** — Return here and press **Next** to retry the connection.

---
💡 **Alternatively**, once configured you will find a **"Pair Bluetooth"** button in the device card that runs these commands automatically.

data_description.printer_name: Leave blank for an automatic name
data_description.encoding: cp437 or cp1252 for most budget thermal printers

error.invalid_mac: Invalid MAC address format. Use AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF
error.invalid_channel: RFCOMM channel must be between 1 and 30
error.device_not_found: Printer not found — check Bluetooth pairing
error.connection_timeout: Timeout — printer out of range or busy
error.bluetooth_unavailable: Bluetooth not available on this host
error.bluetooth_not_supported: Bluetooth Classic (RFCOMM) not supported on this system
error.cannot_connect: Unable to connect to the printer
